name: dockerfile
title: Generate a Dockerfile from a descriptor
description: Describe your app in JSON, get a production-ready Dockerfile.

tabs.label: Output
tabs.content:
FROM node:20-slim

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    curl
 && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /app

COPY package.json package-lock.json* ./
RUN npm ci --omit=dev

COPY . .

ENV NODE_ENV=production

EXPOSE 3000

CMD ["node","server.js"]

name: poetry_migrate
title: Migrate Poetry to uv
description: Convert your `pyproject.toml` from Poetry to uv in one command.

tabs.label: Before
tabs.content:
[tool.poetry]
name = "my_package"
version = "0.1.0"
description = "A test package"
authors = ["Test User <test@example.com>"]

[tool.poetry.dependencies]
python = "^3.9"
requests = "^2.28"

tabs.label: After
tabs.content:
[project]
name = "my_package"
version = "0.1.0"
description = "A test package"
authors = [{ name = "Test User", email = "test@example.com" }]
requires-python = ">=3.9"
dependencies = ["requests>=2.28,<3"]

name: gh_latest
title: Get the latest GitHub release
description: Grab the latest release tag from any GitHub repo — no API token needed.
footer: Pipe it into your scripts, CI, or Dockerfiles. Pair it with `@download` to fetch the asset directly.
